title: Murder in the Maze
description: Murder in the Maze — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: The Air-Rifle
memes.description: The silenced air-rifle used as the murder weapon in the Whistlefield Maze killings — a near-silent projectile weapon modified to deliver curare-tipped darts

memes.label: Alibi
memes.description: The legal defence of having been elsewhere at the time of a crime — a central investigative concept in the Maze murders, particularly regarding the suspects' whereabouts during the attacks

memes.label: Arthur Hawkhurst
memes.description: Arthur Hawkhurst, nephew of Roger Shandon and a beneficiary of his will — an expert airgun marksman whose weapon, skill, and access to curare make him the most obvious suspect in the Maze murders

memes.label: Barrister
memes.description: The legal profession of courtroom advocacy — embodied by Neville Shandon KC, whose barrister's skills drive the Hackleton prosecution and whose absence after his murder reshapes that trial

memes.label: Blackmail
memes.description: The criminal demand for money under threat of exposure — a recurring motive and subplot in the Whistlefield murder case

memes.label: "I: The Hackleton Case"
memes.description: "Neville Shandon stood at the window of his brother’s study gazing contentedly out over the Whistlefield grounds."

memes.label: "II: The Affair in the Maze"
memes.description: "Howard Torrance fidgeted a little and then turned to the girl beside him."

memes.label: "III: The Immediate Results"
memes.description: "As she took shortcuts across the lawns, Vera kept a sharp lookout; but no one was in sight."

memes.label: "IV: The Chief Constable"
memes.description: "As Stenness picked his way through the convolutions of the Maze, his face showed that his mind was at work on some puzzling problem."

memes.label: "V: The Evidence in the Case"
memes.description: "When the doctor had completed his work and left the room, Sir Clinton pulled the tin box of darts from his pocket and went over to the window to examine it."

memes.description: "“The next port of call, Squire, will be Dr."

memes.label: "VII: The Pot of Curare"
memes.description: "After leaving the Chief Constable in the village, Wendover took the road to Whistlefield."

memes.label: "VIII: Opportunity, Method, and Motive"
memes.description: "Wendover picked up the decanter and poured out some whiskey for his guest."

memes.label: "IX: The Burglary at Whistlefield"
memes.description: "When Sir Clinton came down to breakfast on the following morning, Wendover thought that he looked tired and worried, though he was doing his best to show..."

memes.label: "X: The Third Attack in the Maze"
memes.description: "When Sir Clinton came down from his room Wendover noticed that he had mastered his vexation."

memes.label: "XI: The Squire’s Theories"
memes.description: "Wendover nodded."

memes.label: "XII: The Fourth Attack"
memes.description: "“I see the coroner’s jury brought in a verdict of wilful murder against some person or persons unknown,” Wendover remarked."

memes.label: "XIII: The Dart"
memes.description: "The period immediately following the attack upon Sylvia was one of intense inquietude in Wendover’s mind."

memes.label: "XIV: The Forged Cheque"
memes.description: "Sir Clinton had yet another surprise in store for his host."

memes.label: "XV: The Secretary’s Affairs"
memes.description: "Sir Clinton received Ardsley’s news almost as if he had feared that the end was inevitable."

memes.description: "When Ardsley and the Chief Constable entered the other room they found Wendover with Ernest Shandon and Arthur."

memes.label: "XVII: The Siege in the Maze"
memes.description: "Wendover was awakened next morning in an unfamiliar bedroom to find Sir Clinton at his side."

memes.description: "Wendover brought out cigars and cigarettes, and endeavoured to make his guests as comfortable as possible without too much fuss."

memes.label: Clue
memes.description: Physical traces and logical signs that guide investigation — from the poisoned darts to the black thread that becomes the novel's central device of misdirection

memes.label: The Constable at Whistlefield
memes.description: The local police constable who discovers the darts and tin lid at the Maze — his routine search of the crime scene yields the first crucial physical evidence

memes.label: Coroner
memes.description: The judicial officer responsible for investigating sudden or suspicious deaths — the coroner's inquest provides the first official finding on the Shandon murders

memes.label: Costock
memes.description: Costock, a minor character in the Maze murder investigation

memes.label: Cross-Examination
memes.description: The legal process of testing witness testimony through adversarial questioning — a key procedural mechanism in the novel's investigation and its climactic revelations

memes.label: Curare
memes.description: A fast-acting South American arrow poison used to tip the darts fired in the Whistlefield Maze murders — the key forensic discovery that reveals the murder method

memes.label: Darts
memes.description: The curare-tipped projectiles fired from the air-rifle in the Whistlefield Maze murders — the physical evidence discovered at the scene that reveals the killing method

memes.label: Deduction
memes.description: Sir Clinton Driffield's method of logical deduction — reasoning from general principles to specific conclusions — as the intellectual engine of the Maze murder investigation

memes.label: Dr. Ardsley
memes.description: Dr. Ardsley, the local toxicologist whose expertise identifies curare as the poison used in the Maze murders — a grim, clean-shaven man whose vivisection work makes him both essential to the investigation and morally suspect to some characters

memes.label: Dr. Ardsleys Laboratory
memes.description: The research laboratory where Dr. Ardsley conducts his toxicological experiments and vivisection work

memes.label: Ernest Shandon
memes.description: Ernest Shandon, brother of Roger and Neville Shandon, whose broken alibi — exposed through Sir Clinton's bicycle-distance analysis — makes him the investigation's prime suspect and ultimately the identified killer

memes.label: Estate
memes.description: The legal and physical entity of a landed property — Whistlefield functions as both the novel's setting and a contested asset whose disposition drives motive and plot

memes.label: Evidence
memes.description: The physical and testimonial proof gathered during the investigation of the Whistlefield Maze murders — darts, curare, witness statements, and the forensic reasoning that connects them

memes.label: Fingerprint
memes.description: The forensic technique of identifying individuals by their unique finger marks — used by Sir Clinton as a precautionary measure in the Whistlefield investigation

memes.label: Forgery
memes.description: The act of forging a signature on a financial instrument — a plot device that provides motive and backstory for one of the key suspects in the Maze murders

memes.label: Hackleton
memes.description: Hackleton, a figure from the backstory of the Shandon family whose past legal troubles — the "Hackleton case" — provide a backdrop of financial intrigue to the Maze murders

memes.label: Helens Bower
memes.description: One of the two centres of the Whistlefield Maze where Roger Shandon's body is found

memes.label: Howard Torrance
memes.description: Howard Torrance, Roger Shandon's private secretary, whose financial difficulties, involvement in a cheque forgery, and presence near the Maze at the time of the murders make him a significant suspect

memes.label: Jury
memes.description: The panel of citizens who determine facts and return verdicts in English criminal trials — a recurring reference point in the novel's examination of evidence and proof

memes.label: Motive, Means, and Opportunity
memes.description: The classical investigative triad — the three necessary conditions for a crime — that structures Sir Clinton Driffield's methodical investigation of the Maze murders

memes.label: Neville Shandon
memes.description: Neville Shandon, brother of Roger and Ernest Shandon, killed alongside Roger in the Maze — his death as a co-victim rather than the sole target complicates the investigation's understanding of motive

memes.label: Poison
memes.description: The use of poison as a murder method in the Whistlefield Maze killings — a recurring motif in Golden Age detective fiction, here realised through curare delivered by air-rifle dart

memes.label: Pool of Narcissus
memes.description: The second centre of the Whistlefield Maze where Neville Shandon's body is discovered

memes.label: Roger Shandon
memes.description: Roger Shandon, the wealthy owner of Whistlefield estate and the first victim of the Maze murders — a collector of curiosities whose museum contained the curare used to kill him

memes.label: Scotland Yard
memes.description: The London headquarters referenced as the source of Sir Clinton Driffield's investigative authority

memes.label: Scotland Yard
memes.description: The Metropolitan Police headquarters in London, representing official police authority in the Maze murder investigation

memes.label: Sir Clinton Driffield
memes.description: Sir Clinton Driffield, the Chief Constable who investigates the Whistlefield Maze murders — a methodical, scientifically-minded detective whose deductive reasoning drives the investigation

memes.label: Stenness
memes.description: Stenness, a local man intimately familiar with the Whistlefield Maze, who serves as Sir Clinton's guide through the labyrinth and whose personal involvement with one of the suspects adds an emotional dimension to the investigation

memes.label: Suspicion
memes.description: The psychological and investigative dynamics of suspicion — how it gathers around certain characters, shifts between suspects, and is ultimately resolved through rational deduction in the Maze murder case

memes.label: Sylvia Hawkhurst
memes.description: Sylvia Hawkhurst, niece of Roger Shandon, sister of Arthur Hawkhurst, and a beneficiary of Roger's will — a young woman whose romantic entanglements and family position place her at the periphery of the murder investigation

memes.label: The Affair in the Maze
memes.description: The central crime of the novel — the murder of Roger and Neville Shandon by curare-tipped air-rifle darts fired from concealment within the Whistlefield Maze, witnessed aurally by [[vera-forrest|Vera Forrest]] and discovered by [[howard-torrance|Howard Torrance]].

memes.label: The Bicycle Witnesses
memes.description: The witnesses whose accounts of bicycle traffic near Whistlefield help Sir Clinton break Ernest Shandon's alibi

memes.label: The Boat House
memes.description: The boathouse on the riverbank at the edge of the Whistlefield estate, adjacent to the Maze — a critical location for the murderer's escape route, the disposal of the weapon, and the final siege.

memes.label: The Burglary at Whistlefield
memes.description: A burglary that complicates the investigation, potentially linked to the forgery subplot

memes.label: The Butler
memes.description: The butler at Whistlefield estate, whose routine observations — noting who passed the window, who carried what — provide crucial circumstantial evidence in the Maze murder investigation

memes.label: The Chief Constable
memes.description: The local Chief Constable who provides official police support to Sir Clinton Driffield's investigation — a figure of institutional authority who defers to Sir Clinton's superior analytical skills

memes.label: The Coroners Court
memes.description: The location of the inquest into the deaths of Roger and Neville Shandon

memes.label: The Coroners Office
memes.description: The office responsible for conducting the inquest into the deaths of Roger and Neville Shandon

memes.label: The County Police
memes.description: The local county police force that provides constables, conducts searches, and supports Sir Clinton's investigation

memes.label: The Curare Retrieval
memes.description: Sir Clinton's urgent dispatch of Wendover and Ardsley to secure the curare from the Shandon museum

memes.label: The Darts Discovery
memes.description: The constable's discovery of curare-stained darts and a tin lid at the Maze, the first physical evidence

memes.label: The Ernest Shandon Suicide
memes.description: Ernest Shandon's suicide following the exposure of his guilt, closing the case without trial

memes.label: The Final Explanation
memes.description: Sir Clinton's climactic explanation of the entire case to Wendover, Stenness, and Ardsley over dinner

memes.label: The Forged Cheque Discovery
memes.description: The discovery of a forged cheque among Roger Shandon's papers, introducing the financial crime subplot

memes.label: The Fourth Attack
memes.description: Another attack connected to the Maze murder case, further narrowing the suspect pool

memes.label: The Golden Age Detective Story
memes.description: The literary genre to which Murder in the Maze belongs — a tradition of puzzle-centred crime fiction emphasising fair play, rational deduction, and the intellectual contest between reader and detective

memes.label: The Golden Age Detective Writers
memes.description: The community of detective fiction writers to which J.J. Connington belonged, whose conventions shaped the novel

memes.label: The Gun Room
memes.description: The gun room at Whistlefield where Arthur Hawkhurst retrieves his air-rifle before the murders

memes.label: The Hackleton Case
memes.description: The major civil suit for breach of contract in which [[neville-shandon|Neville Shandon]] is lead counsel for the prosecution — a case that threatens to ruin the defendant Hackleton and provides a compelling external motive for Neville's murder in the Whistlefield Maze.

memes.label: The Hawkhurst Family
memes.description: Sylvia and Arthur Hawkhurst, the orphaned niece and nephew of Roger Shandon — equal beneficiaries of his will, residents of Whistlefield, and the siblings whose access to the murder weapon and the curare places them at the centre of the Maze murder investigation

memes.label: The Home Office
memes.description: The government department with ultimate authority over criminal investigations, referenced as Sir Clinton's institutional backing

memes.label: The Inquest
memes.description: The coroner's inquest into the deaths of Roger and Neville Shandon, where witness testimony is formally recorded

memes.label: The Jury at the Inquest
memes.description: The jury empanelled for the coroner's inquest, representing the public's role in legal proceedings

memes.label: The Last Attack in the Maze
memes.description: The final murder attempt in the Maze before the killer is identified
aliases: The Last Attack in the Maze

memes.label: The Legal Profession
memes.description: The solicitors, barristers, and legal officers involved in the administration of Roger Shandon's will and the subsequent legal proceedings

memes.label: The Maze
memes.description: The hedge maze at Whistlefield estate where the Shandon murders take place — central setting and plot device of the novel

memes.label: The Maze Garden
memes.description: The elaborate hedge maze at Whistlefield — scene of the murders and the novel's central location

memes.label: The Medical Profession
memes.description: The doctors, toxicologists, and medical experts whose expertise is essential to the forensic investigation

memes.label: The Museum at Whistlefield
memes.description: Roger Shandon's private museum at Whistlefield — a collection of curiosities from his travels, including the pot of curare that becomes a crucial piece of evidence in the murder investigation

memes.label: The Rhododendron Bank
memes.description: The bank of rhododendrons near the winter garden, used by the murderer as concealment during one attack

memes.label: The Shandon Family
memes.description: The Shandon family of Whistlefield — twin brothers Roger and Neville, the feckless Ernest, and their orphaned niece and nephew Sylvia and Arthur Hawkhurst — whose inheritance, rivalries, and buried secrets supply the motive for the Maze murders

memes.label: The Shandon Museum Room
memes.description: The room at Whistlefield housing Roger Shandon's collection of curiosities, including the curare

memes.label: The Shooting Range
memes.description: The informal shooting range at Whistlefield where Arthur Hawkhurst demonstrates his airgun marksmanship

memes.label: The Siege in the Maze
memes.description: The final confrontation in the Maze, where Sir Clinton's deductions lead to the resolution of the case

memes.label: The Standard Ebooks Editorial Team
memes.description: The editorial team responsible for preparing this edition of the novel, acknowledged in the colophon

memes.label: The Third Attack in the Maze
memes.description: A subsequent attack in the Maze following the initial murders, demonstrating the killer's continued activity
aliases: The Third Attack in the Maze

memes.label: The Toxicology Demonstration
memes.description: Dr. Ardsley's live experiment demonstrating curare's lethal effects, confirming the murder method

memes.label: The Travellers Club
memes.description: Roger Shandon's social circle of fellow travellers, from whose ranks he acquired the curiosities in his museum

memes.label: The Vivisection Licensing Authority
memes.description: The regulatory body that grants vivisection licences, referenced in connection with Dr. Ardsley's research

memes.label: The Will
memes.description: Roger Shandon's last will and testament — the legal document dividing his estate equally among four relatives, establishing the financial context and potential motives for the Maze murders

memes.label: The Winter Garden
memes.description: The winter garden at Whistlefield, adjacent to rhododendron banks, used as a vantage point in one of the attacks

memes.label: The Winter Garden Attack
memes.description: The attack in which Ernest Shandon's suspicious actions in the winter garden are noted

memes.label: Toxicology
memes.description: The forensic science of poisons and their effects — Dr. Ardsley's specialised field, central to identifying curare as the murder weapon in the Whistlefield case

memes.label: Vera Forrest
memes.description: Vera Forrest, a guest at Whistlefield who becomes the key earwitness to the Maze murders — trapped inside the labyrinth while the killer strikes, she hears the sounds that later prove crucial to identifying the murder method

memes.label: Wendover
memes.description: The Squire of Whistlefield, friend and host to Sir Clinton Driffield, who serves as the detective's sounding board, occasional field agent, and the novel's principal audience for the detective's explanations

memes.label: Whistlefield
memes.description: The country estate of Roger Shandon in the English countryside — the primary setting of the novel, encompassing the manor house, gardens, Maze, boathouse, and river frontage where the central murders and investigation unfold.

memes.label: Whistlefield Household Staff
memes.description: The servants at Whistlefield — including the butler — whose observations provide crucial circumstantial evidence

memes.label: Whistlefield Tennis Court
memes.description: The tennis court where the shooting tournament takes place on the day of the murders

memes.label: Whistlefield Village
memes.description: The village near the Shandon estate where the inquest is held and suspects are watched

memes.label: Witness
memes.description: The role of witnesses — those who saw, heard, or observed events related to the Maze murders — in providing testimony that shapes the investigation
